title: Corning (GLW) Q2 Earnings Match Estimates, Revenues Beat
text: Despite management's efforts to improve profitability, Corning's (GLW) revenues fall year over year due to declining net sales in several verticals.

title: Corning (GLW) Q2 Earnings: Taking a Look at Key Metrics Versus Estimates
text: Although the revenue and EPS for Corning (GLW) give a sense of how its business performed in the quarter ended June 2023, it might be worth considering how some key metrics compare with Wall Street estimates and the year-ago numbers.

title: Corning (GLW) Meets Q2 Earnings Estimates
text: Corning (GLW) came out with quarterly earnings of $0.45 per share, in line with the Zacks Consensus Estimate. This compares to earnings of $0.57 per share a year ago.

title: Apple supplier Corning Q3 revenue forecast hit by consumer electronics slump
text: Specialty glass maker Corning forecast third-quarter core sales below Wall Street estimates on Tuesday, as it grapples with the ongoing slump in the consumer electronics market.

title: Corning Reports Second-Quarter 2023 Financial Results with Notable Progress on Profitability and Cash Flow
text: Sales increased sequentially and decreased year over year, in line with management expectations for the current weak end-market environment; Sequential margin expansion and $310 million of free cash flow reflect progress on pricing and productivity actions, with continued improvement expected in the second half; In the third quarter, management expects core sales of approximately $3.5 billion and core EPS to be about the same or slightly better than in the second quarter Sales increased sequentially and decreased year over year, in line with management expectations for the current weak end-market environment; Sequential margin expansion and $310 million of free cash flow reflect progress on pricing and productivity actions, with continued improvement expected in the second half; In the third quarter, management expects core sales of approximately $3.5 billion and core EPS to be about the same or slightly better than in the second quarter

title: Will Revenue Contraction Hinder Corning's (GLW) Q2 Earnings?
text: Corning (GLW) is likely to witness a top-line contraction year over year in second-quarter 2023, owing to sluggish demand trends and macroeconomic headwinds.

title: Pricing Actions To Aid Corning's Q2?
text: Earnings will likely come in at about $0.48 per share, marginally beating the consensus estimate of $0.46.

title: Corning (GLW) Launches Viridian Vials for Sustainable Future
text: Corning's (GLW) Viridian Vials is likely to revolutionize pharmaceutical glass-packaging with significant environmental benefits while meeting the industry's growing demand for efficient production.

title: Corning's New Viridian™ Vials Reduce Waste and Carbon Emissions in the Pharmaceutical Supply Chain
text: The vial technology will help drug manufacturers meet their sustainability goals while enabling faster, safer filling-line operations The vial technology will help drug manufacturers meet their sustainability goals while enabling faster, safer filling-line operations

title: Corning Incorporated: Decent Entry Point For The Long-Term Oriented Investor
text: Corning Incorporated is predicted to continue generating revenue from its Optical Communications segment due to the US government's commitment to expanding rural broadband. The company's Display Technologies segment also holds potential for revenue growth, particularly in the mobile phone and automotive industries. Despite a healthy working capital ratio and interest coverage ratio, Corning's financials are considered underwhelming, with improvements desired in the upcoming quarters. The company's intrinsic value is estimated at $34.64 a share.

title: 4 Dividend-Paying Stocks to Watch Amid Market Uncertainties
text: Here we have shortlisted four dividend-paying tech stocks - AVGO, AVT, TXN and GLW - that may help investors curb the downside risk of the current high-market-volatility environment.

title: Dividend Stocks: Precision Glass Maker Corning Offers Steady Dividend Growth. But What About China?
text: Display technologies account for 23% of revenue, with dividend stock Corning best known for its Gorilla Glass frequently used in smartphones.
